8:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] For the choirmaster. According to Gittith. A Psalm of David. O LORD, our Lord, how majestic is Your name in all the earth! You have set Your glory above the heavens.
8:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] For the Chief Musician; on an instrument of Gath. A Psalm by David. Yahweh, our Lord, how majestic is your name in all the earth! You have set your glory above the heavens!
8:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, concerning the wine-presses, a Psalm of David.
8:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] To the chief Musician upon Gittith, A Psalm of David. O LORD our Lord, how excellent [is] thy name in all the earth! who hast set thy glory above the heavens.

11:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] For the choirmaster. Of David. In the LORD I take refuge. How then can you say to me: “Flee like a bird to your mountain!
11:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] For the Chief Musician. By David. In Yahweh, I take refuge. How can you say to my soul, “Flee as a bird to your mountain”?
11:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, a Psalm of David, upon the eighth.
11:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] To the chief Musician, [A Psalm] of David. In the LORD put I my trust: how say ye to my soul, Flee [as] a bird to your mountain?

14:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] For the choirmaster. Of David. The fool says in his heart, “There is no God.” They are corrupt; their acts are vile. There is no one who does good.
14:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] For the Chief Musician. By David. The fool has said in his heart, “There is no God.” They are corrupt. They have done abominable deeds. There is no one who does good.
14:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. O Lord, who shall sojourn in thy tabernacle? and who shall dwell in thy holy mountain?
14:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] To the chief Musician, [A Psalm] of David. The fool hath said in his heart, [There is] no God. They are corrupt, they have done abominable works, [there is] none that doeth good.

15:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. O LORD, who may abide in Your tent? Who may dwell on Your holy mountain?
15:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm by David. Yahweh, who shall dwell in your sanctuary? Who shall live on your holy hill?
15:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A writing of David. Keep me, O Lord; for I have hoped in thee.
15:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. LORD, who shall abide in thy tabernacle? who shall dwell in thy holy hill?

16:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Miktam of David. Preserve me, O God, for in You I take refuge.
16:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Poem by David. Preserve me, God, for I take refuge in you.
16:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Prayer of David. Hearken, O Lord of my righteousness, attend to my petition; give ear to my prayer not [uttered] with deceitful lips.
16:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] Michtam of David. Preserve me, O God: for in thee do I put my trust.

17:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A prayer of David. Hear, O LORD, my righteous plea; listen to my cry. Give ear to my prayer— it comes from lips free of deceit.
17:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Prayer by David. Hear, Yahweh, my righteous plea. Give ear to my prayer that doesn’t go out of deceitful lips.
17:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, [a Psalm] of David the servant of the Lord; [the words] which he spoke to the Lord, [even] the words of this Song, in the day in which the Lord delivered him out of the hand of all his enemies, and out of the hand of Saul: and he said:
17:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Prayer of David. Hear the right, O LORD, attend unto my cry, give ear unto my prayer, [that goeth] not out of feigned lips.

23:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
23:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm by David. Yahweh is my shepherd; I shall lack nothing.
23:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm for David on the first day of the week. The earth is the Lord's and the fulness thereof; the world, and all that dwell in it.
23:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. The LORD [is] my shepherd; I shall not want.

24:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. The earth is the LORD’s, and the fullness thereof, the world and all who dwell therein.
24:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm by David. The earth is Yahweh’s, with its fullness; the world, and those who dwell in it.
24:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. To thee, O Lord, have I lifted up my soul.
24:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. The earth [is] the LORD’s, and the fulness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein.

25:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] Of David. To You, O LORD, I lift up my soul;
25:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] By David. To you, Yahweh, I lift up my soul.
25:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of David. Judge me, O Lord; for I have walked in my innocence: and hoping in the Lord I shall not be moved.
25:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of David. Unto thee, O LORD, do I lift up my soul.

26:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] Of David. Vindicate me, O LORD! For I have walked with integrity; I have trusted in the LORD without wavering.
26:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] By David. Judge me, Yahweh, for I have walked in my integrity. I have trusted also in Yahweh without wavering.
26:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of David, before he was anointed. The Lord is my light and my Saviour; whom shall I fear? the Lord is the defender of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?
26:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of David. Judge me, O LORD; for I have walked in mine integrity: I have trusted also in the LORD; [therefore] I shall not slide.

27:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] Of David. The LORD is my light and my salvation— whom shall I fear? The LORD is the stronghold of my life— whom shall I dread?
27:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] By David. Yahweh is my light and my salvation. Whom shall I fear? Yahweh is the strength of my life. Of whom shall I be afraid?
27:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of David. To thee, O Lord, have I cried; my God, be not silent toward me: lest thou be silent toward me, and so I should be likened to them that go down to the pit.
27:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of David. The LORD [is] my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? the LORD [is] the strength of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?

28:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] Of David. To You, O LORD, I call; be not deaf to me, O my Rock. For if You remain silent, I will be like those descending to the Pit.
28:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] By David. To you, Yahweh, I call. My rock, don’t be deaf to me, lest, if you are silent to me, I would become like those who go down into the pit.
28:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David [on the occasion] of the solemn assembly of the Tabernacle. Bring to the Lord, ye sons of God, bring to the Lord young rams; bring to the Lord glory and honour.
28:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of David. Unto thee will I cry, O LORD my rock; be not silent to me: lest, [if] thou be silent to me, I become like them that go down into the pit.

29:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. Ascribe to the LORD, O heavenly beings, ascribe to the LORD glory and strength.
29:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm by David. Ascribe to Yahweh, you sons of the mighty, ascribe to Yahweh glory and strength.
29:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, a Psalm and Song at the dedication of the house of David.
29:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. Give unto the LORD, O ye mighty, give unto the LORD glory and strength.

30:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm. A song for the dedication of the temple. Of David. I will exalt You, O LORD, for You have lifted me up and have not allowed my foes to rejoice over me.
30:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm. A Song for the Dedication of the Temple. By David. I will extol you, Yahweh, for you have raised me up, and have not made my foes to rejoice over me.
30:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, a Psalm of David, [an utterance] of extreme fear.
30:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm [and] Song [at] the dedication of the house of David. I will extol thee, O LORD; for thou hast lifted me up, and hast not made my foes to rejoice over me.

32:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] Of David. A Maskil. Blessed is he whose transgressions are forgiven, whose sins are covered.
32:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] By David. A contemplative psalm. Blessed is he whose disobedience is forgiven, whose sin is covered.
32:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of David. Rejoice in the Lord, ye righteous; praise becomes the upright.
32:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of David, Maschil. Blessed [is he whose] transgression [is] forgiven, [whose] sin [is] covered.

34:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] Of David, when he pretended to be insane before Abimelech, so that the king drove him away. I will bless the LORD at all times; His praise will always be on my lips.
34:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] By David; when he pretended to be insane before Abimelech, who drove him away, and he departed. I will bless Yahweh at all times. His praise will always be in my mouth.
34:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of David. Judge thou, O Lord, them that injure me, fight against them that fight against me.
34:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of David, when he changed his behaviour before Abimelech; who drove him away, and he departed. I will bless the LORD at all times: his praise [shall] continually [be] in my mouth.

35:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] Of David. Contend with my opponents, O LORD; fight against those who fight against me.
35:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] By David. Contend, Yahweh, with those who contend with me. Fight against those who fight against me.
35:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, by David the servant of the Lord.
35:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of David. Plead [my cause], O LORD, with them that strive with me: fight against them that fight against me.

36:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] For the choirmaster. A Psalm of David, the servant of the LORD. An oracle is in my heart regarding the transgression of the wicked man: There is no fear of God before his eyes.
36:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] For the Chief Musician. By David, the servant of Yahweh. A revelation is within my heart about the disobedience of the wicked: There is no fear of God before his eyes.
36:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of David. Fret not thyself because of evil-doers, neither be envious of them that do iniquity.
36:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] To the chief Musician, [A Psalm] of David the servant of the LORD. The transgression of the wicked saith within my heart, [that there is] no fear of God before his eyes.

37:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] Of David. Do not fret over those who do evil; do not envy those who do wrong.
37:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] By David. Don’t fret because of evildoers, neither be envious against those who work unrighteousness.
37:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David for remembrance concerning the Sabbath-day.
37:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of David. Fret not thyself because of evildoers, neither be thou envious against the workers of iniquity.

45:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] For the choirmaster. To the tune of “The Lilies.” A Maskil of the sons of Korah. A love song. My heart is stirred by a noble theme as I recite my verses to the king; my tongue is the pen of a skillful writer.
45:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] For the Chief Musician. Set to “The Lilies.” A contemplation by the sons of Korah. A wedding song. My heart overflows with a noble theme. I recite my verses for the king. My tongue is like the pen of a skillful writer.
45:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, for the sons of Core; a Psalm concerning secret things.
45:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] To the chief Musician upon Shoshannim, for the sons of Korah, Maschil, A Song of loves. My heart is inditing a good matter: I speak of the things which I have made touching the king: my tongue [is] the pen of a ready writer.

50:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of Asaph. The Mighty One, God the LORD, speaks and summons the earth from where the sun rises to where it sets.
50:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm by Asaph. The Mighty One, God, Yahweh, speaks, and calls the earth from sunrise to sunset.
50:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, a Psalm of David,
50:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of Asaph. The mighty God, [even] the LORD, hath spoken, and called the earth from the rising of the sun unto the going down thereof.

56:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] For the choirmaster. To the tune of “A Dove on Distant Oaks.” A Miktam of David, when the Philistines seized him in Gath. Be merciful to me, O God, for men are hounding me; all day they press their attack.
56:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Silent Dove in Distant Lands.” A poem by David, when the Philistines seized him in Gath. Be merciful to me, God, for man wants to swallow me up. All day long, he attacks and oppresses me.
56:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end. Destroy not: by David, for a memorial, when he fled from the presence of Saul to the cave.
56:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] To the chief Musician upon Jonath-elem-rechokim, Michtam of David, when the Philistines took him in Gath. Be merciful unto me, O God: for man would swallow me up; he fighting daily oppresseth me.

57:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] For the choirmaster. To the tune of “Do Not Destroy.” A Miktam of David, when he fled from Saul into the cave. Have mercy on me, O God, have mercy, for in You my soul takes refuge. In the shadow of Your wings I will take shelter until the danger has passed.
57:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Do Not Destroy.” A poem by David, when he fled from Saul, in the cave. Be merciful to me, God, be merciful to me, for my soul takes refuge in you. Yes, in the shadow of your wings, I will take refuge, until disaster has passed.
57:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end. Destroy not: by David, for a memorial.
57:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] To the chief Musician, Al-taschith, Michtam of David, when he fled from Saul in the cave. Be merciful unto me, O God, be merciful unto me: for my soul trusteth in thee: yea, in the shadow of thy wings will I make my refuge, until [these] calamities be overpast.

59:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] For the choirmaster. To the tune of “Do Not Destroy.” A Miktam of David, when Saul sent men to watch David’s house in order to kill him. Deliver me from my enemies, O my God; protect me from those who rise against me.
59:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Do Not Destroy.” A poem by David, when Saul sent, and they watched the house to kill him. Deliver me from my enemies, my God. Set me on high from those who rise up against me.
59:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, for them that shall yet be changed; for an inscription by David for instruction,
59:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] To the chief Musician, Al-taschith, Michtam of David; when Saul sent, and they watched the house to kill him. Deliver me from mine enemies, O my God: defend me from them that rise up against me.

65:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] For the choirmaster. A Psalm of David. A song. Praise awaits You, O God, in Zion; to You our vows will be fulfilled.
65:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. A song. Praise waits for you, God, in Zion. Vows shall be performed to you.
65:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, a Song of a Psalm of resurrection. Shout unto God, all the earth.
65:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] To the chief Musician, A Psalm [and] Song of David. Praise waiteth for thee, O God, in Sion: and unto thee shall the vow be performed.

66:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] For the choirmaster. A song. A Psalm. Make a joyful noise to God, all the earth!
66:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] For the Chief Musician. A song. A Psalm. Make a joyful shout to God, all the earth!
66:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, a Psalm of David among the Hymns.
66:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] To the chief Musician, A Song [or] Psalm. Make a joyful noise unto God, all ye lands:

73:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of Asaph. Surely God is good to Israel, to those who are pure in heart.
73:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm by Asaph. Surely God is good to Israel, to those who are pure in heart.
73:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of instruction for Asaph. Wherefore hast thou rejected [us], O God, for ever? [wherefore] is thy wrath kindled against the sheep of thy pasture?
73:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of Asaph. Truly God [is] good to Israel, [even] to such as are of a clean heart.

74:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Maskil of Asaph. Why have You rejected us forever, O God? Why does Your anger smolder against the sheep of Your pasture?
74:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A contemplation by Asaph. God, why have you rejected us forever? Why does your anger smolder against the sheep of your pasture?
74:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, Destroy not, a Psalm of a Song for Asaph.
74:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] Maschil of Asaph. O God, why hast thou cast [us] off for ever? [why] doth thine anger smoke against the sheep of thy pasture?

76:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] For the choirmaster. With stringed instruments. A Psalm of Asaph. A song. God is known in Judah; His name is great in Israel.
76:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] For the Chief Musician. On stringed instruments. A Psalm by Asaph. A song. In Judah, God is known. His name is great in Israel.
76:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, for Idithun, a Psalm of Asaph.
76:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] To the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm [or] Song of Asaph. In Judah [is] God known: his name [is] great in Israel.

77:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] For the choirmaster. According to Jeduthun. A Psalm of Asaph. I cried out to God; I cried aloud to God to hear me.
77:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] For the Chief Musician. To Jeduthun. A Psalm by Asaph. My cry goes to God! Indeed, I cry to God for help, and for him to listen to me.
77:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of instruction for Asaph. Give heed, O my people, to my law: incline your ear to the words of my mouth.
77:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] To the chief Musician, to Jeduthun, A Psalm of Asaph. I cried unto God with my voice, [even] unto God with my voice; and he gave ear unto me.

78:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Maskil of Asaph. Give ear, O my people, to my instruction; listen to the words of my mouth.
78:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A contemplation by Asaph. Hear my teaching, my people. Turn your ears to the words of my mouth.
78:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm for Asaph. O God, the heathen are come into thine inheritance; they have polluted thy holy temple; they have made Jerusalem a storehouse of fruits.
78:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] Maschil of Asaph. Give ear, O my people, [to] my law: incline your ears to the words of my mouth.

79:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of Asaph. The nations, O God, have invaded Your inheritance; they have defiled Your holy temple and reduced Jerusalem to rubble.
79:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm by Asaph. God, the nations have come into your inheritance. They have defiled your holy temple. They have laid Jerusalem in heaps.
79:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, for alternate [strains], a testimony for Asaph, a Psalm concerning the Assyrian.
79:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of Asaph. O God, the heathen are come into thine inheritance; thy holy temple have they defiled; they have laid Jerusalem on heaps.

80:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] For the choirmaster. To the tune of “The Lilies of the Covenant.” A Psalm of Asaph. Hear us, O Shepherd of Israel, who leads Joseph like a flock; You who sit enthroned between the cherubim, shine forth
80:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “The Lilies of the Covenant.” A Psalm by Asaph. Hear us, Shepherd of Israel, you who lead Joseph like a flock, you who sit above the cherubim, shine out.
80:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, a Psalm for Asaph, concerning the wine-presses.
80:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] To the chief Musician upon Shoshannim-eduth, A Psalm of Asaph. Give ear, O Shepherd of Israel, thou that leadest Joseph like a flock; thou that dwellest [between] the cherubims, shine forth.

81:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] For the choirmaster. According to Gittith. Of Asaph. Sing for joy to God our strength; make a joyful noise to the God of Jacob.
81:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] For the Chief Musician. On an instrument of Gath. By Asaph. Sing aloud to God, our strength! Make a joyful shout to the God of Jacob!
81:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm for Asaph. God stands in the assembly of gods; and in the midst [of them] will judge gods.
81:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] To the chief Musician upon Gittith, [A Psalm] of Asaph. Sing aloud unto God our strength: make a joyful noise unto the God of Jacob.

82:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of Asaph. God presides in the divine assembly; He renders judgment among the gods:
82:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm by Asaph. God presides in the great assembly. He judges among the gods.
82:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of a Psalm for Asaph.
82:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of Asaph. God standeth in the congregation of the mighty; he judgeth among the gods.

84:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] For the choirmaster. According to Gittith. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. How lovely is Your dwelling place, O LORD of Hosts!
84:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] For the Chief Musician. On an instrument of Gath. A Psalm by the sons of Korah. How lovely are your dwellings, Yahweh of Armies!
84:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, a Psalm for the sons of Core.
84:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] To the chief Musician upon Gittith, A Psalm for the sons of Korah. How amiable [are] thy tabernacles, O LORD of hosts!

86:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A prayer of David. Incline Your ear, O LORD, and answer me, for I am poor and needy.
86:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Prayer by David. Hear, Yahweh, and answer me, for I am poor and needy.
86:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of a Song for the sons of Core. His foundations are in the holy mountains.
86:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Prayer of David. Bow down thine ear, O LORD, hear me: for I [am] poor and needy.

87:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of the sons of Korah. A song. He has founded His city on the holy mountains.
87:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm by the sons of Korah; a Song. His foundation is in the holy mountains.
87:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A song of a Psalm for the sons of Core for the end, upon Maeleth for responsive [strains], of instruction for Æman the Israelite.
87:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm [or] Song for the sons of Korah. His foundation [is] in the holy mountains.

90:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A prayer of Moses the man of God. Lord, You have been our dwelling place through all generations.
90:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Prayer by Moses, the man of God. Lord, you have been our dwelling place for all generations.
90:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] Praise of a Song, by David. He that dwells in the help of the Highest, shall sojourn under the shelter of the God of heaven.
90:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Prayer of Moses the man of God. Lord, thou hast been our dwelling place in all generations.

98:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm. Sing to the LORD a new song, for He has done wonders; His right hand and holy arm have gained Him the victory.
98:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm. Sing to Yahweh a new song, for he has done marvelous things! His right hand and his holy arm have worked salvation for him.
98:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. The Lord reigns;—let the people rage; [it is he] that sits upon the cherubs, let the earth be moved.
98:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm. O sing unto the LORD a new song; for he hath done marvellous things: his right hand, and his holy arm, hath gotten him the victory.

100:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of thanksgiving. Make a joyful noise to the LORD, all the earth.
100:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of thanksgiving. Shout for joy to Yahweh, all you lands!
100:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. I will sing to thee, O Lord, of mercy and judgment; I will sing a psalm,
100:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of praise. Make a joyful noise unto the LORD, all ye lands.

101:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. I will sing of Your loving devotion and justice; to You, O LORD, I will sing praises.
101:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm by David. I will sing of loving kindness and justice. To you, Yahweh, I will sing praises.
101:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Prayer for the Poor; when he is deeply afflicted, and pours out his supplication before the Lord.
101:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. I will sing of mercy and judgment: unto thee, O LORD, will I sing.

102:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A prayer of one who is afflicted, when he grows faint and pours out his lament before the LORD. Hear my prayer, O LORD; let my cry for help come before You.
102:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Prayer of the afflicted, when he is overwhelmed and pours out his complaint before Yahweh. Hear my prayer, Yahweh! Let my cry come to you.
102:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of David. Bless the Lord, O my soul; and all [that is] within me, [bless] his holy name.
102:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Prayer of the afflicted, when he is overwhelmed, and poureth out his complaint before the LORD. Hear my prayer, O LORD, and let my cry come unto thee.

109:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] For the choirmaster. A Psalm of David. O God of my praise, be not silent.
109:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. God of my praise, don’t remain silent,
109:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. The Lord said to my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool.
109:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. Hold not thy peace, O God of my praise;

110:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. The LORD said to my Lord: “Sit at My right hand until I make Your enemies a footstool for Your feet.”
110:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm by David. Yahweh says to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies your footstool for your feet.”
110:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] Alleluia. I will give thee thanks, O Lord, with my whole heart, in the council of the upright, and [in] the congregation.
110:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool.

120:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A song of ascents. In my distress I cried to the LORD, and He answered me.
120:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Ascents. In my distress, I cried to Yahweh. He answered me.
120:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Degrees. I lifted up mine eyes to the mountains, whence my help shall come.
120:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of degrees. In my distress I cried unto the LORD, and he heard me.

121:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A song of ascents. I lift up my eyes to the hills. From where does my help come?
121:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Ascents. I will lift up my eyes to the hills. Where does my help come from?
121:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Degrees. I was glad when they said to me, Let us go into the house of the Lord.
121:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of degrees. I will lift up mine eyes unto the hills, from whence cometh my help.

122:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A song of ascents. Of David. I was glad when they said to me, “Let us go to the house of the LORD.”
122:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Ascents. By David. I was glad when they said to me, “Let’s go to Yahweh’s house!”
122:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Degrees. Unto thee who dwellest in heaven have I lifted up mine eyes.
122:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of degrees of David. I was glad when they said unto me, Let us go into the house of the LORD.

123:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A song of ascents. I lift up my eyes to You, the One enthroned in heaven.
123:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Ascents. I lift up my eyes to you, you who sit in the heavens.
123:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Degrees. If it had not been that the Lord was among us, let Israel now say;
123:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of degrees. Unto thee lift I up mine eyes, O thou that dwellest in the heavens.

124:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A song of ascents. Of David. If the LORD had not been on our side— let Israel now declare—
124:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Ascents. By David. If it had not been Yahweh who was on our side, let Israel now say,
124:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Degrees. They that trust in the Lord [shall be] as mount Sion: he that dwells in Jerusalem shall never be moved.
124:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of degrees of David. If [it had not been] the LORD who was on our side, now may Israel say;

125:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A song of ascents. Those who trust in the LORD are like Mount Zion. It cannot be moved; it abides forever.
125:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Ascents. Those who trust in Yahweh are as Mount Zion, which can’t be moved, but remains forever.
125:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Degrees. When the Lord turned the captivity of Sion, we became as comforted ones.
125:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of degrees. They that trust in the LORD [shall be] as mount Zion, [which] cannot be removed, [but] abideth for ever.

126:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A song of ascents. When the LORD restored the captives of Zion, we were like dreamers.
126:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Ascents. When Yahweh brought back those who returned to Zion, we were like those who dream.
126:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Degrees. Except the Lord build the house, they that build labour in vain: except the Lord keep the city, the watchman watches in vain.
126:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of degrees. When the LORD turned again the captivity of Zion, we were like them that dream.

127:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A song of ascents. Of Solomon. Unless the LORD builds the house, its builders labor in vain; unless the LORD protects the city, its watchmen stand guard in vain.
127:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Ascents. By Solomon. Unless Yahweh builds the house, they who build it labor in vain. Unless Yahweh watches over the city, the watchman guards it in vain.
127:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Degrees. Blessed are all they that fear the Lord; who walk in his ways.
127:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of degrees for Solomon. Except the LORD build the house, they labour in vain that build it: except the LORD keep the city, the watchman waketh [but] in vain.

128:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A song of ascents. Blessed are all who fear the LORD, who walk in His ways!
128:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Ascents. Blessed is everyone who fears Yahweh, who walks in his ways.
128:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Degrees. Many a time have they warred against me from my youth, let Israel now say:
128:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of degrees. Blessed [is] every one that feareth the LORD; that walketh in his ways.

129:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A song of ascents. Many a time they have persecuted me from my youth— let Israel now declare—
129:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Ascents. Many times they have afflicted me from my youth up. Let Israel now say:
129:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Degrees. Out of the depths have I cried to thee, O Lord.
129:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of degrees. Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth, may Israel now say:

130:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A song of ascents. Out of the depths I cry to You, O LORD!
130:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Ascents. Out of the depths I have cried to you, Yahweh.
130:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Degrees. O Lord, my heart is not exalted, neither have mine eyes been [haughtily] raised: neither have I exercised myself in great [matters], nor in things too wonderful for me.
130:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of degrees. Out of the depths have I cried unto thee, O LORD.

132:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A song of ascents. O LORD, remember on behalf of David all the hardships he endured,
132:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Ascents. Yahweh, remember David and all his affliction,
132:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Degrees. See now! what is so good, or what so pleasant, as for brethren to dwell together?
132:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of degrees. LORD, remember David, [and] all his afflictions:

133:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A song of ascents. Of David. Behold, how good and pleasant it is when brothers live together in harmony!
133:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Ascents. By David. See how good and how pleasant it is for brothers to live together in unity!
133:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of Degrees. Behold now, bless ye the Lord, all the servants of the Lord, who stand in the house of the Lord, in the courts of the house of our God.
133:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Song of degrees of David. Behold, how good and how pleasant [it is] for brethren to dwell together in unity!

138:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] Of David. I give You thanks with all my heart; before the gods I sing Your praises.
138:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] By David. I will give you thanks with my whole heart. Before the gods, I will sing praises to you.
138:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, a Psalm of David. O Lord, thou hast proved me, and known me.
138:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of David. I will praise thee with my whole heart: before the gods will I sing praise unto thee.

139:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] For the choirmaster. A Psalm of David. O LORD, You have searched me and known me.
139:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. Yahweh, you have searched me, and you know me.
139:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] For the end, a Psalm of David.
139:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. O LORD, thou hast searched me, and known [me].

141:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. I call upon You, O LORD; come quickly to me. Hear my voice when I call to You.
141:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm by David. Yahweh, I have called on you. Come to me quickly! Listen to my voice when I call to you.
141:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of instruction for David, when he was in the cave,—a Prayer.
141:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] A Psalm of David. LORD, I cry unto thee: make haste unto me; give ear unto my voice, when I cry unto thee.

144:0.bsb: [v1, includes superscription] Of David. Blessed be the LORD, my Rock, who trains my hands for war, my fingers for battle.
144:0.web: [v1, includes superscription] By David. Blessed be Yahweh, my rock, who trains my hands to war, and my fingers to battle—
144:0.brenton: [v1, includes superscription] David's [Psalm of] praise. I will exalt thee, my God, my king; and I will bless thy name for ever and ever.
144:0.kjv: [v1, includes superscription] [A Psalm] of David. Blessed [be] the LORD my strength, which teacheth my hands to war, [and] my fingers to fight:
